    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/ssl3con.c", "symbol": "ssl3_ComputeMasterSecret"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/ssl3con.c", "symbol": "ssl3_DeriveConnectionKeys"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/ssl3con.c", "symbol": "ssl3_HandleClientHelloPart2"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/ssl3con.c", "symbol": "ssl3_HandleServerHelloPart2"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/tls13con.c", "symbol": "tls13_SetCipherSpec"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/tls13con.c", "symbol": "tls13_SendServerHelloSequence"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/tls13con.c", "symbol": "tls13_ComputeHandshakeSecrets"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/tls13con.c", "symbol": "tls13_ComputeApplicationSecrets"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/tls13con.c", "symbol": "tls13_HandleClientHelloPart2"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/dtlscon.c", "symbol": "dtls_TLSVersionToDTLSVersion"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/dtlscon.c", "symbol": "dtls_DTLSVersionToTLSVersion"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/dtlscon.c", "symbol": "dtls_StartRetransmitTimer"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/ssl3ecc.c", "symbol": "ssl_NamedGroup2ECParams"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8", "path": "security/nss/lib/ssl/ssl3ecc.c", "symbol": "ssl_ECPubKey2NamedGroup"}
  ],
  "text": "Firefox NSS TLS/SSL Implementation Audit Report\n\n1. SSL3CON.C (github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8/security/nss/lib/ssl/ssl3con.c)\n   Primary Protocol: SSL 3.0/TLS Record Processing and Cipher Suite Negotiation\n   Description: Implements core SSL 3.0 and TLS (versions 1.0-1.2) protocol handling including record-layer processing, handshake message parsing, and cipher suite negotiation. Key functions include ssl3_GenerateRSAPMS for RSA pre-master secret generation, ssl3_ComputeMasterSecret for deriving the master secret, ssl3_DeriveConnectionKeys for key derivation, ssl3_HandleClientHelloPart2 and ssl3_HandleServerHelloPart2 for handshake message processing, and functions for sending certificates, finished messages, and server key exchange messages.\n\n2. TLS13CON.C (github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8/security/nss/lib/ssl/tls13con.c)\n   Primary Protocol: TLS 1.3 Handshake and Record Processing\n   Description: Implements TLS 1.3 protocol with updated handshake flow and record processing. Key functions include tls13_SetCipherSpec for installing cipher specifications for each epoch, tls13_SendServerHelloSequence for coordinating server-side handshake responses, tls13_ComputeHandshakeSecrets and tls13_ComputeApplicationSecrets for deriving TLS 1.3 specific secrets using HKDF, tls13_HandleClientHelloPart2 for processing client hello messages, and functions for handling key shares, encrypted extensions, certificates, and finished messages.\n\n3. DTLSCON.C (github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8/security/nss/lib/ssl/dtlscon.c)\n   Primary Protocol: DTLS-Specific Protocol Handling\n   Description: Implements Datagram TLS (DTLS) specific protocol functionality including version mapping between TLS and DTLS wire formats, retransmission timers, and DTLS-specific constraints. Key functions include dtls_TLSVersionToDTLSVersion and dtls_DTLSVersionToTLSVersion for converting between TLS and DTLS version numbers, dtls_StartRetransmitTimer, dtls_RetransmitTimerExpiredCb, dtls_SendSavedWriteData, and dtls_CancelAllTimers for managing DTLS-specific retransmission behavior over unreliable UDP transport.\n\n4. SSL3ECC.C (github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8/security/nss/lib/ssl/ssl3ecc.c)\n   Primary Protocol: ECC Cipher Suite Operations\n   Description: Implements Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) operations for SSL/TLS cipher suites. Originally moved from ssl3con.c to isolate ECC-specific functionality. Key functions include ssl_NamedGroup2ECParams for converting named elliptic curve groups to ASN.1 DER-encoded EC parameters, and ssl_ECPubKey2NamedGroup for extracting the named curve from an EC public key.\n\n5. SSLEXP.H (github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8/security/nss/lib/ssl/sslexp.h)\n   Primary Protocol: Experimental SSL APIs\n   Description: Header file declaring experimental and forward-compatible SSL APIs that are not guaranteed to remain stable across NSS versions. Provides abstractions for custom extension handling through SSL_GetExtensionSupport and SSL_InstallExtensionHooks, allowing applications to install custom TLS extension writers and handlers. Defines the SSL_EXPERIMENTAL_API macro for safe invocation of experimental functions."
}

# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

**Target Repositories (version-pinned mirrors):**

- `github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8` — use `repo:^github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8$` filter

Scope ALL keyword_search/nls_search queries to these repos.
Use the repo name as the `repo` parameter for read_file/go_to_definition/find_references.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Firefox NSS TLS Implementation Audit

## Your Task

Audit the TLS/SSL implementation in Firefox's Network Security Services (NSS) library. Find all C source files in `mozilla-firefox/firefox` under `security/nss/lib/ssl/` that implement core TLS protocol handling: 1. The file that implements SSL 3.0/TLS record processing and cipher suite negotiation (`ssl3con.c`). 2. The file that implements TLS 1.3 handshake and record processing (`tls13con.c`). 3. The file that implements DTLS-specific protocol handling (`dtlscon.c`). 4. The file that implements ECC cipher suite operations (`ssl3ecc.c`). 5. The header file that declares experimental SSL APIs (`sslexp.h`). Report the repo, file path, and the primary protocol function or feature each file implements.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

No local repositories are pre-checked out.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/firefox--871325b8` (mozilla-firefox/firefox)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you audit the TLS/SSL implementation in Firefox's NSS library. Let me start by exploring the repository structure to locate the specific files you mentioned.
Perfect! I found all the files. Now let me read each of these files to understand their primary protocol functions and features.
Now let me search for some key functions to understand better what primary protocols/features each file implements.
Let me search for more specific functions to better understand the primary features of each file.
Now I have a complete understanding of these files. Let me create the answer.json file with my findings.
Let me verify the answer file was created correctly:

Perfect! I've successfully completed the Firefox NSS TLS implementation audit. Here's a summary of my findings:

## Audit Results

I found all 5 requested C source/header files in the NSS library under `security/nss/lib/ssl/`:

1. **ssl3con.c** - Implements SSL 3.0/TLS record processing and cipher suite negotiation, including key derivation and handshake message handling

2. **tls13con.c** - Implements TLS 1.3 handshake and record processing with modern cryptographic primitives and secret derivation using HKDF

3. **dtlscon.c** - Implements DTLS-specific protocol handling for datagram transport, including version mapping and retransmission timers

4. **ssl3ecc.c** - Implements ECC cipher suite operations for elliptic curve cryptography support in SSL/TLS

5. **sslexp.h** - Header file declaring experimental SSL APIs for custom TLS extension handling

The complete audit report has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with:
- All 5 files listed with repo and path information
- Key symbolic functions from each file
- Detailed narrative descriptions of each file's primary protocol features and implementations

Key functions include ssl3_GenerateRSAPMS for RSA pre-master secret generation, ssl3_ComputeMasterSecret for deriving the master secret, ssl3_DeriveConnectionKeys for key derivation, ssl3_HandleClientHelloPart2 and ssl3_HandleServerHelloPart2 for handshake message processing, and functions for sending certificates, finished messages, and server key exchange messages.\n\n2. TLS13CON.C (github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8/security/nss/lib/ssl/tls13con.c)\n   Primary Protocol: TLS 1.3 Handshake and Record Processing\n   Description: Implements TLS 1.3 protocol with updated handshake flow and record processing. Key functions include tls13_SetCipherSpec for installing cipher specifications for each epoch, tls13_SendServerHelloSequence for coordinating server-side handshake responses, tls13_ComputeHandshakeSecrets and tls13_ComputeApplicationSecrets for deriving TLS 1.3 specific secrets using HKDF, tls13_HandleClientHelloPart2 for processing client hello messages, and functions for handling key shares, encrypted extensions, certificates, and finished messages.\n\n3. Key functions include dtls_TLSVersionToDTLSVersion and dtls_DTLSVersionToTLSVersion for converting between TLS and DTLS version numbers, dtls_StartRetransmitTimer, dtls_RetransmitTimerExpiredCb, dtls_SendSavedWriteData, and dtls_CancelAllTimers for managing DTLS-specific retransmission behavior over unreliable UDP transport.\n\n4. SSL3ECC.C (github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8/security/nss/lib/ssl/ssl3ecc.c)\n   Primary Protocol: ECC Cipher Suite Operations\n   Description: Implements Elliptic Curve Cryptography (ECC) operations for SSL/TLS cipher suites. Originally moved from ssl3con.c to isolate ECC-specific functionality. Key functions include ssl_NamedGroup2ECParams for converting named elliptic curve groups to ASN.1 DER-encoded EC parameters, and ssl_ECPubKey2NamedGroup for extracting the named curve from an EC public key.\n\n5. SSLEXP.H (github.com/sg-evals/firefox--871325b8/security/nss/lib/ssl/sslexp.h)\n   Primary Protocol: Experimental SSL APIs\n   Description: Header file declaring experimental and forward-compatible SSL APIs that are not guaranteed to remain stable across NSS versions. Provides abstractions for custom extension handling through SSL_GetExtensionSupport and SSL_InstallExtensionHooks, allowing applications to install custom TLS extension writers and handlers. Defines the SSL_EXPERIMENTAL_API macro for safe invocation of experimental functions."
}
